next_steps: Perform image-guided biopsy of the retroperitoneal or mediastinal soft-tissue masses
Request pathology review with IgG4 immunostaining
Complete an autoimmune and inflammatory panel (including ANCA and ANA)
Consult cardiology to monitor for malignant arrhythmias given the prolonged QTc
Consider PET/CT scan to assess systemic involvement and target biopsy sites
Initiate high-dose glucocorticoid therapy pending biopsy results if hemodynamically stable
Image-guided biopsy of the retroperitoneal or mediastinal soft-tissue mass for histopathology and immunohistochemical staining
Water deprivation test or plasma/urine osmolality evaluation to investigate potential diabetes insipidus
Skeletal survey or review of existing CT imaging for long-bone osteosclerosis characteristic of Erdheim-Chester disease
Cardiac consultation with continuous telemetry to manage the prolonged QTc and monitor for arrhythmia
PET/CT scan to assess total body disease extent and guide biopsy site selection
Serum and urine protein electrophoresis with immunofixation to rule out systemic amyloidosis or plasma cell dyscrasias
